metadata.summary: Four-phase Product Hunt launch: build 500+ email list (4-8 weeks), secure niche hunter (1000+ followers), launch 12:01 AM PT Tue-Thu, reply 1 hour.

content.title: Product Hunt Launch System
content.summary: Four-phase Product Hunt launch system: build 500+ email list (4-8 weeks), secure niche hunter (1000+ followers), launch 12:01 AM PT Tue-Thu, reply within 1 hour. Never ask for upvotes directly.

why.p: Upvote velocity in the first few hours determines the final ranking. Accounts with no pre-built audience or weak listing assets consistently fail because Product Hunt is a social product: early supporters with prior product relationships drive the initial spike that earns Explore placement. The tagline must be under 60 characters and benefit-focused ("Build beautiful landing pages in minutes" not "AI-Powered Landing Page Generator"). A dedicated `/welcome-producthunters` landing page with a custom offer and separate tracking separates PH conversion from organic traffic.

list.item: Product is polished and launch-ready; need all listing assets (tagline variants, first comment, gallery descriptions, launch email, social posts).
Evaluating launch readiness against the 4-week checklist before committing to a date.
Drafting canned responses for the 5 most likely comment types in advance.
Post-launch: generating a retrospective for IndieHackers from metrics and comment thread.
Building a hunter outreach message or waitlist page copy.
Product is not yet functional or has major UX issues — public launch damages credibility permanently.
Email list under 200 warm contacts — build audience first; launch date can wait.
Planning to launch on a weekend or during a major tech event — timing is non-negotiable.
Expecting automated upvoting, fake comments, or inauthentic support coordination.
Assuming PH traffic alone sustains growth after day 1 — plan post-launch nurture.

section.name: phase-1-prepare
section.p: Preparation timeline and prerequisites: 4-8 weeks before launch.

rule.statement: Build an email list of 500+ interested users before launch. Without warm contacts, launch support is insufficient for early upvote velocity.
rule.rationale: Upvote velocity in the first hours determines the final ranking. Cold launches with no pre-built audience consistently fail to rank because they have no initial signal.

rule.statement: Secure a hunter with 1,000+ followers in the same niche. Hunter audience overlap with your product category matters more than raw follower count.
rule.rationale: A niche-relevant hunter's followers are more likely to upvote and comment because the product matches their interests. A large generic hunter provides less relevant traffic.

rule.statement: Do not launch if the product has major UX issues or is not functional. A PH launch surfaces all problems publicly and damages credibility permanently.
rule.rationale: PH comments are indexed and permanent. Negative launch comments about bugs or poor UX will follow the product indefinitely and reduce trust for future prospects.

rule.statement: Tagline must be under 60 characters, benefit-focused, and contain no jargon. Validate length programmatically after generation — agents frequently exceed the limit.
rule.rationale: The tagline is the primary hook for PH visitors scanning the feed. Over-limit taglines are truncated. Jargon excludes non-technical visitors who are valid early adopters.

rule.statement: Write the first comment before launch day. Have it ready to paste within 60 seconds of the post going live — early velocity is critical.
rule.rationale: First comments on PH posts receive the most visibility. A prepared maker comment with backstory, differentiators, and a special offer sets the tone for all subsequent engagement.

rule.statement: Tagline formula: [Action verb] [outcome] [for whom] [differentiator]. Under 60 characters. Validate length after generation.
rule.rationale: Action verbs signal what the product does immediately. Outcome communicates value. "For whom" targets the right audience. Under 60 characters prevents truncation in the feed.

example.title: Strong tagline with all elements
example.description: Tagline: "Build beautiful landing pages in minutes" — action verb (Build), outcome (beautiful landing pages), timeframe as differentiator (in minutes). 43 characters.

example.title: Weak tagline without user action
example.description: Tagline: "AI-Powered Landing Page Generator for Modern Businesses" — no action verb for the user, "AI-Powered" is jargon, too long (55 chars but generic). Does not communicate user outcome.

list.item: Image 1: Hero shot with tagline overlay
Images 2-4: Key features with one-sentence explanations
Image 5: Testimonials or social proof numbers
Image 6: CTA or pricing summary

rule.statement: Do not mention unfinished features in the listing or comments. PH commenters will test everything mentioned; negative comments about missing features compound quickly.
rule.rationale: PH comments are permanent and indexed. Overpromising on launch day creates immediate negative social proof that follows the product indefinitely.

list.item: 12:01 AM PT: Post goes live, first comment posted immediately
6:00 AM PT: Send launch email to supporter list
9:00 AM PT: Post on social media channels
12:00 PM PT: Engage with all comments
3:00 PM PT: Second social push
6:00 PM PT: Thank you post, community outreach
9:00 PM PT: Final engagement push before ranking closes

rule.statement: Respond to EVERY comment within 1 hour. Never ask for upvotes directly — this is against PH TOS and looks desperate.
rule.rationale: Active maker engagement is a strong positive signal on PH. Accounts with high comment response rates rank better and are perceived as more trustworthy.

list.item: Within 24 hours: thank supporters, share ranking and stats, collect testimonials from commenters
Within 1 week: post retrospective on IndieHackers, nurture new email subscribers, follow up with interested users
Long-term: add "Featured on Product Hunt" badge, include in press kit, use ranking as social proof

antipattern.description: Launching without a pre-built email list or community.
antipattern.reason: Without warm contacts to drive early upvote velocity, the listing never reaches the first page. Building the list before launch is more important than the launch date itself.

antipattern.description: Launching on a weekend or during a major tech event.
antipattern.reason: Weekends have fewer active PH users; major events dominate attention. Tuesdays through Thursdays have the highest active user counts and lowest competition.

antipattern.description: First comment drafted as corporate marketing copy.
antipattern.reason: PH community values authenticity. A polished, corporate first comment reduces maker credibility. Write in first-person casual voice and review for authenticity before posting.

antipattern.description: Asking for upvotes directly or including "upvote us" or "share with your network" in launch copy.
antipattern.reason: Asking for upvotes directly is a PH TOS violation and, if flagged, can result in removal of the listing. Let the product quality drive upvotes organically.

section.name: agent-integration-and-limitations
section.p: An agent is best used in two phases: (1) pre-launch asset generation — given product description, target audience, and key differentiators, produce the tagline, gallery descriptions, first comment, launch email, and social posts; (2) post-launch analysis — given the comment thread export and traffic/signup metrics, produce a retrospective with lessons learned and follow-up actions. On launch day itself, the agent can draft real-time comment responses if given the incoming comment text, but a human must post them. Allow at least 2 hours of human availability on launch day for comment moderation.

Agents cannot post to Product Hunt or monitor the listing in real-time; human presence is mandatory on launch day. Image and video assets require human design or dedicated design tools — agents can specify requirements but cannot generate compliant visuals. Upvote velocity is a social phenomenon; agent-generated copy improves conversion but cannot guarantee ranking. HN and Reddit cross-posts are separate tactics; PH launch alone does not guarantee sustained traffic after day 1. Product Hunt's algorithm changes; guidance may lag current ranking factors.

Agents may generate taglines that exceed the 60-character limit; always validate length programmatically after generation. First comment drafts often sound too polished/corporate — instruct agent to write in first-person casual voice and review for authenticity before posting. Agents cannot monitor real-time upvote velocity or detect if the post is being flagged — human must watch the dashboard. Human-in-loop checkpoint: agent drafts responses to comments; human reviews and posts — never allow fully automated comment posting on a public platform. Launch day email timing is critical; agent-generated send schedules should be validated against the subscriber timezone distribution before execution. Agents may include calls to action that violate PH TOS ("upvote us", "share with your network") — explicitly forbid this in the system prompt.

Best practices: Request the hunt from a hunter with 1,000+ followers in the same niche; hunter audience overlap with your product category matters more than raw follower count. Prepare canned responses for the 5 most likely comment types (how does X work, what's the pricing, is there a free trial, how does it compare to Y, can I use it for Z) — agent can draft these in advance. Do not use launch day to announce unfinished features; HN/PH commenters will test everything mentioned and negative comments compound fast. Add a dedicated landing page URL for PH traffic (e.g., `/welcome-producthunters`) with a custom offer and tracking — measure conversion separately from organic traffic. Post the retrospective on IndieHackers within 48 hours while the launch is fresh — it doubles as marketing and community building.

template.purpose: PH first comment: maker intro, differentiators, special offer, roadmap teaser.
Launch day email to supporters: link, upvote CTA, no-pressure framing.
Agent prompt: 5 tagline variants + first comment + launch email.
Validate proposed launch datetime: must be 12:01 AM PT, Tue-Thu.
